I treated the prior investigations as a proposal log. The prompt fixes the four-generator tame format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma = tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and verifier exponents modulo M=85667662080.The prior record decisively refutes the early scalar and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 candidates; the only live proposal is the Turn 9‚Äď20 dynamic tame-inertia word.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2 and ő∑=2ŌÄ on prime-to-2 tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑=‚ąí2ŌÄ ‚Č°50932630783(modM).This candidate is still not proved, but it is the only one not yet killed by a finite-tame or index-two test.The important correction this turn is about the remaining Frobenius-action gap. The Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg method for pÓÄ†=2 is not merely ‚Äúcheck DemuŇ°kin kernels‚ÄĚ; Neukirch describes that proof as checking the DemuŇ°kin group over every finite tame normal subextension together with detailed action data, then using a Koch/DemuŇ°kin formation uniqueness theorem.Numdam Jarden‚ÄďShusterman likewise note that the explicit Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg description is for pÓÄ†=2, while Diekert‚Äôs dyadic analogue does not cover K=Q2.TAU Mathematics Labute‚Äôs classification explains why the dyadic orientation character is part of the DemuŇ°kin type, not optional.Cambridge University Press & Assessment I specialized the live candidate to the unramified quadratic quotient, where ŌĄ=1 and ŌÄ acts as 1 on the pro-2 unramified direction. The relation becomesr=xŌÉ‚ąí1 xŌÉ[ŌÉ,y].

Let U=ker(ŌÉ‚Ü¶C2), put c=S=ŌÉ2, and use Schreier generatorsx0=x,x1=xŌÉ,y0=y,y1=yŌÉ.

The two Schreier relators arer0=x0S‚ąí1x1Sy1y0‚ąí1,r1=x1x0Sy0S‚ąí1y1‚ąí1.

Now seta0=x0,a1=x1,b=y0x1,c=S.

Solving r1 for y1 gives an exact formula for conjugation by ŌÉ, call it őĪ, on the four-generator kernel:őĪ(a0)őĪ(a1)őĪ(b)őĪ(c)=a1,=ca0c‚ąí1,=a1a0cba1‚ąí1a0c‚ąí1,=c.(‚ėÖ)This is the most concrete object I extracted this turn. It packages the entire 2-primary Frobenius action selected by the live candidate in the first hard case. Any final proof must compare this exact filtered automorphism with the arithmetic Frobenius action, not only with its degree-one, cup-product, Bockstein, or U/U3 shadows.This also changes how I read Turns 19‚Äď20. Their corrected classesőīn (b)=z[2n],őīn(a0)=[a1,(adz)2n‚ąí1c],őīn(a1)=[a0,(adz)2n‚ąí1c],with z=[a0,a1], are valid linearized symplectic derivation classes: they preserve the initial DemuŇ°kin relatorq=a0[2]+a1[2]+[b,c]to first order. But they should not be treated as independent actual Frobenius-action choices without solving the nonlinear deformation equations.

For example, at n=0,őī0 (b)=z,őī0(a0)=[a1,c],őī0(a1)=[a0,c],őī0 (c)=0,and the linear calculation gives őī0 (q)=0. However the naive map v‚Ü¶v+őī0 (v) does not preserve the restricted relation strictly: after the first-order cancellation, the degree-four residual term is[a1,c][2]+[a0,c][2].This term is not formally zero. A quick representation check in gl2(F2) sendsa0‚Ü¶0,a1‚Ü¶E21,c‚Ü¶E12,b‚Ü¶0,so q‚Ü¶0, but[a1,c][2]+[a0,c][2]‚Ü¶I2ÓÄ†=0.Thus Turn 20‚Äôs tower is real as a tangent-space obstruction, but it is not yet a tower of freely adjustable profinite extensions. The correct deformation problem is a Maurer‚ÄďCartan-style recursive problem: first-order cocycles such as őīn must be accompanied by higher corrections. The exact candidate automorphism (‚ėÖ) gives one full recursive solution.So the current state is sharper than Turn 20: the live candidate is still unrefuted, but the remaining proof should not try to match isolated őīn ‚Äôs one by one as independent arithmetic invariants. The next useful target is to compute the associated graded expansion of the single exact automorphism (‚ėÖ) and compare that whole recursive solution with Frobenius on explicit Kummer/Witt data over the unramified quadratic field.

ESTABLISHED: The live Turn 9‚Äď20 candidate remains unrefuted. In the unramified quadratic specialization, I derived the exact Frobenius actionőĪ(a0)=a1,őĪ(a1)=ca0c‚ąí1,őĪ(b)=a1a0cba1‚ąí1a0c‚ąí1,őĪ(c)=c.

I also showed that Turn 20‚Äôs őīn -tower is only linearized data: already őī0 has a nonzero degree-four nonlinear residual unless higher corrections are supplied.

GAP: The candidate is still not a proved presentation of GQ2. The remaining gap is to compare the exact filtered Frobenius automorphism (‚ėÖ), or its full recursive graded expansion, with the actual arithmetic Frobenius action. DemuŇ°kin type, orientation, and finite low-depth checks still do not by themselves prove the full profinite presentation.

NEXT: Expand (‚ėÖ) in the Zassenhaus/restricted-Lie filtration through degrees 4 and 5, identify which higher correction cancels the residual [a1,c][2]+[a0,c][2], and compare that correction with explicit arithmetic Kummer/Witt data over the unramified quadratic extension of Q2.[BREAKTHROUGH]
